column83: It was great doing intakes with caring and supportive LCSW’s but I suggest being more transparent with availability of services individuals are reporting they’d like to access.  For my son he’s in remission now and does  not want to engage with cancer programs for emotional reasons. So though programs are available now we were clear we wanted emotional support and massage to help him  his chemo treatments. I understand needs are high and a non profits ability to deliver programs can be limited. Thank you for all that you do. Xoxo

column83: The Dempsey Center was not really promoted to us at the beginning of treatment. I wish there were more PR from Dempsey to all of the oncology offices/treatment facilities outside of Portland/Lewiston. It felt like our oncology team did not know enough about Dempsey and services provided. They didn't promote it b/c they weren't educated about all of the goodness that is the Dempsey Center. The midcoast seems to be in the dark about all of the services and about the virtual component. I've been spreading the word, but would love to shout it from the rooftops and get providers and patients educated! Maybe there could be in-person presentations taken to the hospitals/oncology offices? Thank you for all you do!

column83: This service was incredibly important for myself and family as we were in the thick of a cancer diagnosis and knowing that we were being treated for the cancer, but emotionally, we were struggling. The support and services that we each received was invaluable. It helped us through those early dark days and showed us that brighter days are ahead. The staff at the front desk was so helpful and positive when we came to the office in South Portland. So grateful for Dempsey Center and the services that they offer to those going through this diagnosis.

column83: The Dempsey Center has been a Godsend to me walking my own journey of breast cancer along with being a caretaker to my mother walking her own terminal lung cancer diagnosis.The Dempsey Center programs have provided me with "calm in my storm" through 2023, forever grateful.
It is a Joy to be a member!
Keep up the good work. It is a great service and does so much good for people. Most grateful.

column83: I love the Dempsey Center... It's always a very up experience for me going there. The services have all had an positive impact on my attitude and recovery, from cancer diagnosis through treatments.  Yoga, Reiki and Acupuncture have all helped me in the process, and I hope to continue as I am at a 'wait & see' stage and need to maintain a healthy mental outlook. Thank you for being there. Without Dempsey I believe I would surely have a feeling of hopelessness... but with Dempsey, I feel more hopeful about my future years...

When my husband's brain cancer started growing again. The Dempsey center was able to provide him with services quickly. We live in NH and could not find the support we needed right away. I so appreciate having the video we made at your center as a way to remember my husband. It was often difficult to get to Portland was my husband grew weaker. A zoom session might have been helpful but realized the counselor needed to have a NH certification. We were so grateful to have the support of the Dempsey Center to help us through a my husband's eventual death from his cancer.

column83: I just wish there was some way that I can express my gratitude. I'll start with a simply thank you from the bottom of my heart. Without this program, I honestly do not think I would have made so many gains with my emotional state of mind. Finding others that know exactly what I'm talking about made all of the difference in the world. Being able to attend from home made all of the difference as well. Someday I'll be able to give back and pay it forward and the Dempsey Center is the top of my list. Thank you so very, very much.

column83: I am incredibly grateful for my time at the Dempsey Center. Reiki, acupuncture, and massage were crucial to my recovery. These made me able to relax and function even tho I was very stressed out and sore. My cancer was no big deal compared to others and I am/was very conscious of that. I was never on chemo or radiation.  But no one at the Center made me self conscious about it. Thank you so much.

column83: It was a surprise for me to find out how valuable, in my life with this chronic disease, are the services that I continue to use through the Dempsey Center.  I meet with Kerry Irish, by zoom, for private counseling which has helped me feel more powerful, optimistic, and able to communicate with family and friends. Also, I was NOT going to join a support group, but on her recommendation to just try it, I did join, and after these months with our groups weekly zoom meetings, I feel the support they lend, the humor, suggestions, sharing of grief, and the support I can give back, has been remarkable. I am so grateful for Kerry's guidance and I share to others that The Dempsey Center offers so much that no one else has. I applaud the center.

column83: 98% supportive and client centered services.  I did have one very negative interaction on an intial visit with an acupuncturist.  The coordinator heard of it, called me and listened.  She summarized this interaction was against the primary Dempsey purpose of always respecting and taking their lead from the client.    I let some time go by as it was very upsetting but ultimatey benefited from another provider.    One suggestion:   Clarify when and if it is expected to notify someone when you are unable to attend a session of a virtual session you are registered to attend.  I've received differing answers.  It is amazing at a time of uncertainty,  the Center is able to provide acceptance, support and such a variety of services that  unbeknownst to   them brought me from a dark place to life.  The positive calm demeanor of volunteers and paid staff is amazing.   Thank you to all!    BYW I hope there are supportive services to all staff as I'm sure they deal with extremely difficult situations and emotional goodbyes.

column83: The Dempsey Center has been incredibly helpful to me during a difficult diagnosis. I have been taking multiple online fitness and meditation classes for almost a year, have had consults with Tish and Judy when surgery or other factors impacted my nutrition or fitness needs, and therapy with Christine is an INCREDIBLE gift--I adore her!    I'm glad support groups work for others, but when my care partner and I tried them, we found them stressful in the face of my own uncertain prognosis, because people were often sharing dire news and it was hard to hear and anxiety-producing. Because I am in "watch and wait" mode, I'm not sure if I'm a candidate for a Survivorship program or not, but even if I am, I'm a wary of joining one because if I hear a lot of stories of difficulty, it can be activating for me at a time when I'm trying to find calm. I'm not sure what to do about this, and it's obviously my own problem, but I'm thinking that maybe some one-on-one coaching for survivors that could be more tailored to individual needs would be amazing. For example, I am struggling with emotional issues--anxiety, fear, trauma--because of a sudden diagnosis at a young age and a situation that doesn't lend itself to an NED (because the spots seen on the scans are too small to be biopsied, but also don't seem to be growing or changing), but I'm not having any physical impacts from my treatment (which I know is very lucky!). So it would be tough to be in a Survivorship group with people who had more conclusive results OR were dealing with physical impacts I'm fortunate to not be experiencing.    All that said, I am SO grateful to the Dempsey and feel so lucky that we have this incredible resource here in this state. I really don't know what I would do without it.
